mapping.a_element: effective Polyakov-loop models for SU(3) Yang-Mills theory
mapping.b_element: dimensionally reduced effective theory for SU(2) Yang-Mills theory respecting center symmetry

mapping.a_element: inverse Monte Carlo procedure based on Schwinger-Dyson equations to determine effective couplings
mapping.b_element: perturbative matching to the full theory followed by non-perturbative lattice simulations

mapping.a_element: first-order phase transition in SU(3) Yang-Mills theory
mapping.b_element: second-order confining phase transition in SU(2) Yang-Mills theory

mapping.a_element: symmetries of the Haar measure used in Schwinger-Dyson equations
mapping.b_element: center symmetry incorporated in the effective theory

mapping.a_element: fine-tuning problem due to first-order transition
mapping.b_element: applicability of dimensional reduction near the confinement transition

brief_justification: While both papers construct effective theories for Yang-Mills theories and study phase transitions, the core mechanisms differ: Paper A uses an inverse Monte Carlo procedure with Schwinger-Dyson equations to derive effective couplings, focusing on a first-order transition and fine-tuning issues. Paper B employs perturbative matching and lattice simulations to study a second-order transition driven by center symmetry, with no shared mathematical machinery for determining effective parameters or handling transitions.
